name: EcoFlow DELTA 2 Portable Power Station
verdict: The best overall portable power station — 1024Wh LiFePO4, charges in 50 minutes to 80%, and X-Boost handles appliances up to 2200W.
description: The EcoFlow DELTA 2 is the power station reviewers keep recommending. Its X-Stream technology charges from 0 to 80% in just 50 minutes via wall outlet — no other station in this class comes close. X-Boost technology lets it power appliances drawing up to 2200W despite a rated 1800W output, meaning it handles everything from full-size fridges to power tools. The LiFePO4 battery is rated for 3,000+ cycles, and the EcoFlow app provides real-time monitoring and control. At 27 lbs, it's portable enough for car camping while powerful enough for home backup. Outdoor Life and GearLab's top pick.
features: X-Boost: powers appliances up to 2200W on 1800W rated
cons: Fan can be loud during fast charging

name: Jackery Explorer 1000 v2 Portable Power Station
verdict: The most user-friendly power station — 1070Wh LiFePO4, 1-hour fast charge, and Jackery's famously simple interface.
description: Jackery is the Apple of portable power — everything just works without reading a manual. The Explorer 1000 v2 upgrades to LiFePO4 chemistry with 4,000+ cycle life, charges to full in just 1 hour with emergency mode via the app, and weighs only 23.8 lbs with a foldable handle. Three AC outlets, USB-C PD 100W, and a 12V car port cover every charging scenario. The interface is dead simple — a clear LCD shows input/output watts and remaining time. Jackery's #1 best-seller on Amazon for good reason.
features: Simple LCD with remaining time display
pros: Easiest to use — no learning curve
cons: 1500W output is lower than EcoFlow DELTA 2
No dedicated app monitoring on base model
Solar charging is slower than competitors

name: Anker SOLIX C1000 Portable Power Station
verdict: The best value power station — 1056Wh LiFePO4 with 2000W output for under $400 on sale.
description: The Anker SOLIX C1000 Gen 2 delivers flagship specs at a mid-range price. 1056Wh of LiFePO4 capacity, 2000W continuous output (2400W surge), and a 58-minute wall charge time make it competitive with stations costing $200 more. GearLab rated it 'Best for Most People' thanks to its balance of price, power, and portability. The Anker app provides remote monitoring and firmware updates. At frequently-on-sale prices under $400, it's the best value in the 1000Wh class. 5-year warranty from one of the most trusted names in charging.
features: 2000W output — highest in its price class
pros: Best value — flagship specs under $400 on sale
cons: App can be slow to connect

name: BLUETTI EB3A Portable Power Station
verdict: The best budget power station — 268Wh LiFePO4 for under $200 with 600W output.
description: The BLUETTI EB3A is the entry point to LiFePO4 portable power. At $199, it packs 268Wh of capacity, 600W continuous output, and charges from 0-80% in 30 minutes via wall outlet. It won't run a fridge, but it handles laptops, phones, CPAP machines, drones, and small appliances easily. The built-in wireless charging pad on top is a nice touch. At just 10.1 lbs, it's genuinely portable — throw it in a backpack for camping or keep it in a closet for emergencies. Outdoor Life's 'Best Budget' pick.
pros: Cheapest LiFePO4 power station worth buying
Wireless charging pad is a nice bonus
cons: Fan can be loud for its size

name: Jackery Explorer 300 Portable Power Station
verdict: The lightest entry-level power station — 293Wh at just 7.1 lbs, perfect for camping and device charging.
description: The Jackery Explorer 300 is the power station for people who value portability above all else. At 7.1 lbs, it's lighter than most laptops and small enough to fit in a daypack. 293Wh of capacity and 300W output handle phones, laptops, cameras, drones, and LED lights with ease. It won't power heavy appliances, but that's not its job — it's the ultimate device charger for camping, road trips, and emergency phone charging. Amazon's #1 best-selling portable power station with 7,000+ units sold monthly.
cons: 300W output limits what you can power

name: EcoFlow DELTA Pro 3 Portable Power Station
verdict: The best power station for whole-home backup — 4kWh capacity, expandable to 48kWh, and can wire directly into your breaker panel.
description: The DELTA Pro 3 is less a 'portable' power station and more a home energy system that happens to be movable. With 4,000Wh of LiFePO4 capacity (expandable to a staggering 48kWh with extra batteries), 4000W output, and the ability to wire directly into your home breaker panel via an optional transfer switch, this is serious home backup power. It handles everything — fridges, HVAC, sump pumps, medical equipment. The EcoFlow app provides detailed energy management, and solar input accepts up to 2600W for off-grid living. At $1,699, it's an investment, but it replaces a gas generator permanently.
features: Wires into breaker panel via transfer switch
pros: Can replace a gas generator permanently
Wires into home panel — no extension cords

name: Goal Zero Yeti 700 Portable Power Station
verdict: The most rugged power station — built for harsh conditions with Goal Zero's legendary durability.
description: Goal Zero pioneered portable solar power, and the Yeti 700 carries that legacy forward. 697Wh of lithium-ion capacity and 600W output handle camping, overlanding, and emergency use. What sets it apart is build quality — the Yeti feels like a tool, not an appliance. The stackable design lets you daisy-chain multiple units, and the integrated MPPT solar controller maximizes efficiency from Goal Zero's solar panels. It's heavier than competitors (18.3 lbs for its capacity) and lacks LiFePO4, but for users who prioritize durability and the Goal Zero ecosystem, it's the right choice. Outdoor Life's 'Most Reliable' pick.
cons: Heavier than competitors for its capacity

name: EcoFlow RIVER 2 Portable Power Station
verdict: The cheapest EcoFlow worth buying — 256Wh LiFePO4 with X-Boost for $149.
description: The RIVER 2 brings EcoFlow's technology to the budget tier. At $149, you get 256Wh of LiFePO4 capacity, 300W output with X-Boost to handle devices up to 600W, and a 60-minute wall charge. At just 7.7 lbs, it's barely heavier than the Jackery 300 but with superior battery chemistry. The EcoFlow app works here too, providing monitoring and settings. For phone/laptop charging, camping lights, and small device power, it's the best budget option from a premium brand.
pros: Cheapest LiFePO4 from a premium brand

name: BLUETTI AC200L Portable Power Station
verdict: The best mid-range workhorse — 2048Wh LiFePO4 with 2400W output at under $1,000.
description: The BLUETTI AC200L is the power station you buy when 1000Wh isn't enough but $1,700 for an EcoFlow DELTA Pro 3 is too much. 2048Wh of LiFePO4 capacity and 2400W continuous output (3600W surge) powers everything from fridges to power tools to window AC units. Dual charging lets you combine wall + solar for faster recharging. The built-in wireless charging pad and 7 AC outlets cover every scenario. At $999, it's the most capacity per dollar in the premium tier. 62 lbs means it stays in one place, but that's the tradeoff for this much power.
pros: Best capacity per dollar in premium tier
7 AC outlets is most in class
cons: Slow wall charge without dual input
BLUETTI app is basic vs EcoFlow

name: GRECELL T3000 Portable Power Station
verdict: The best power-per-dollar budget option — 2880Wh LiFePO4 with 3000W output for under $500.
description: GRECELL isn't a household name, but the T3000 offers specs that make the big brands nervous. 2880Wh of LiFePO4 capacity and 3000W output for under $500 — that's roughly 3x the capacity of the Anker C1000 at a similar price. The catch is build quality and software aren't at EcoFlow or Jackery levels, and at 71.4 lbs it's essentially stationary. But for home backup on a budget, there's nothing else that delivers this much power for this little money. 3,500+ cycle LiFePO4 battery means it'll last a decade.
features: UPS function for seamless power switching
